baseline.commit_subject: Add Mira Core Phase 19 encryption key validation
baseline.baseline_boundary: Phase 19 defines encryption/key-reference/secret-handling validation only. No real Mira Core server, API listener, route table, network path, database/store write, queue, auth/session/keygen/encrypt/decrypt/KMS/env-secret read, local execution, send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, export, deletion, restore, raw-content storage, or output-file behavior exists at this baseline.

purpose: Convert the next server-runtime blocker into an executable validation contract for future Mira Core server API/control-plane/status behavior before any real API server exists. Phase 20 defines dependency mapping for Phases 14-19, planned endpoint and control-plane shapes, request/response envelope shapes, status semantics, operator controls, privacy/security boundaries, capability boundaries, migration gates, blockers, static rules, acceptance checks, and side-effect truth.
nonGoals: No database, source-store, or file write.
No auth, token, credential, secret, or session change.
No environment secret read or export.
No browser, window, screenshot, or OCR access.
No external sends or customer sends.

scope: Build validation-only server API/control-plane/status assessment records against this fixture, if Builder is later delegated.

Do not start a server, bind a listener, register routes, make network calls, write databases/stores/files, run migrations, create queues, mutate auth/session/key/secret state, generate keys, encrypt, decrypt, call KMS, read env secrets, execute locally, send, deploy, trade, commit memory/profile state, export a file, delete records, restore records, or store raw content.
Represent commit 8a8ccf0 as the Phase 19 baseline and map the green dependency chain for Phases 14-19.

Emit dependency map, planned endpoint/control-plane shape, request/response envelope rules, status semantics, operator controls, privacy/security boundaries, capability boundaries, migration gates, gate summary, blocker summary, evidence refs, and side-effect truth sections.

Represent endpoints as planned shapes only for receive upload, auth/signature/key validation, store dry-run status, export/delete status, retention/tombstone status, local-arms/offline status, intent proposal status, audit-log status, and health/readiness status.

Represent API outputs as redacted status/envelope records with no raw payload, no secrets, no bearer tokens, no cookies, no private/data keys, no plaintext, no raw ciphertext, and no decrypted content.

Treat API/status validity as validation-only. It never authorizes DB writes, storage, local execution, shell/PTY, Builder/Oracle direct server targeting, Tier 2+ work, customer sends, deploys, trades, memory/profile commits, model-processing proof, bridge green, raw restore, or resurrection of deleted local state.
hardRejects: Any server process, listener, route registration, network request, database/store/file write, migration execution, queue write, auth/secret/token/session/key change, key generation, encryption, decryption, KMS call, env secret read, local execution, shell/PTY, browser/window access, send, customer send, deploy, trade, financial action, durable migration, memory/profile commit, export-file write, deletion, restore, raw-content storage, or output-file write occurs.

Any fixture or validation output contains raw comms, raw terminal, screenshot/OCR, browser state, customer private content, secret material, side-profile content, profile-mismatch payloads, bearer tokens, cookies, session secrets, private keys, data keys, plaintext, raw ciphertext, raw payloads, decrypted content, or raw database payloads.

A planned endpoint/control-plane entry implies a live HTTP server, route, listener, websocket, API call, database write, queue write, job, worker, delete/export action, deploy, send, trade, local execution, or output file.

An API status marks socket connection as bridge green, delivery acceptance as model-processing proof, or a lease/status response as recipient model processing proof without recipient quote-back or equivalent local proof.

A request/response envelope accepts missing scope, missing auth binding ref, missing signature ref, missing key reference ref, missing source watermark, missing idempotency/replay key, raw payload, expired request, replay, tombstone conflict, profile mismatch, side-profile payload, or high-risk action as allowed.

Server API validity is treated as authorization for DB writes, storage, local execution, shell/PTY, direct Builder/Oracle targeting, Tier 2+, customer sends, deploys, trades, memory/profile commits, raw restore, or resurrection of deleted state.

Phase 20 migration language implies a real API server can exist before transport/TLS, auth middleware, replay store, rate limits, audit logging, schema migrations, encryption/KMS integration, deletion/export jobs, operator UI, monitoring/alerts, disaster recovery, profile isolation tests, and red-team leakage tests are reviewed.

staticValidationRules.must: server_api_assessment.baseline_commit and validation_report.baseline_commit are exactly 8a8ccf0, the Phase 19 encryption-key baseline.

Phase 20 emits validation records only; it does not start a server, bind listeners, register routes, perform network work, write stores/files, run migrations, create queues, mutate auth/session/key state, generate keys, encrypt, decrypt, call KMS, read env secrets, execute locally, send, deploy, trade, commit memory/profile data, export, delete, restore, store raw content, or write output files.
Dependency map includes Phases 14-19 with fixture paths, commits, green status, and validation-only boundaries.

Planned endpoints cover receive upload envelope, auth/signature/key validation, store dry-run status, export/delete status, retention/tombstone status, local-arms/offline status, intent proposal status, audit log status, and health/readiness status without creating real routes.

Request/response envelopes require method/path/action_id, idempotency/replay keys, tenant/profile/device/session scope, auth_binding_ref, signature_ref, key_reference_ref, source watermark, redaction summary, evidence refs, risk tier, status, allowed flag, review_required, expires_at, and no raw payload.

Allowed statuses include validation-only acceptance, blocked, pending local acceptance, offline local arms, review required, expired, replay rejected, tombstone wins, no store performed, and no execution performed.
Operator controls are read-only status/report controls; future delete/export/retention controls are request records and redacted manifests/status only, not real actions.

API outputs contain no raw comms, terminal, OCR, browser, customer private, secret, side-profile, profile-mismatch, bearer token, cookie, session secret, private key, data key, plaintext, raw ciphertext, raw payload, or decrypted content.
Idempotency and replay keys are required; replay and expired requests fail closed, and tombstones win over stale or conflicting requests.
Server API/status may report local arms offline or unknown but cannot claim local execution, PTY, shell, browser/window access, bridge green, or model-processing proof.

API validity/status does not authorize DB writes, storage, local execution, shell/PTY, Builder/Oracle direct server target, Tier 2+ action, customer-send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, model-processing proof, bridge green, raw restore, or server resurrection of deleted local state.
Tier 3 and Tier 4 actions, customer sends, deploys, trades, financial actions, auth/secret changes, and local execution remain blocked or require review, never accepted as API-allowed work.

Real API server remains blocked until transport/TLS, auth middleware, replay store, rate limits, audit logging, schema migrations, encryption/KMS integration, deletion/export jobs, operator UI, monitoring/alerts, disaster recovery, profile isolation tests, and red-team leakage tests exist.
All Phase 20 side-effect truth fields are true/zero-equivalent in assessment and validation report.
All required Phase 20 acceptance gate IDs and blocker IDs are present and unique.

Assessment and validation output contain no secret-like strings, raw private content, bearer tokens, cookies, keys, plaintext/ciphertext/decrypted content, server/listener/network/write claims, bridge/proof overclaims, direct Builder/Oracle target authorization, high-risk authorization claims, or live side-effect claims.

summaryAcceptanceCriteria: Phase 20 maps Phases 14-19 as green validation-only dependencies and does not claim any dependency is a real server/API/runtime.

Phase 20 is validation/spec-only and performs no runtime, server, listener, route, network, DB/store/file write, migration, queue, auth/session mutation, key generation, encryption, decryption, KMS, env secret read, local execution, send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, export, deletion, restore, raw-content storage, or output-file side effect.
Server API output shape includes server_api_assessment and validation_report.

Planned endpoint/control-plane shape covers receive upload envelope, auth/signature/key-ref validation, future store dry-run status, export/delete request status, retention/tombstone status, local-arms/offline status, intent proposal status, audit log status, and health/readiness status.

Request/response envelopes require method, path, action_id, idempotency key, replay key, tenant/profile/device/session scope, auth binding ref, signature ref, key reference ref, source watermark, redaction summary, evidence refs, risk tier, status, allowed flag, review_required, expires_at, and no raw payload.

Status semantics include accepted_for_validation_only, blocked, pending_local_acceptance, offline_local_arms, review_required, expired, replay_rejected, tombstone_wins, no_store_performed, and no_execution_performed.
Operator controls are read-only status/report controls; future delete/export/retention controls are represented as request records and redacted manifests/status only.

API outputs block raw comms, terminal, OCR, browser, customer private, secret, side-profile, profile mismatch, bearer token, cookie, session secret, private key, data key, plaintext, raw ciphertext, raw payload, and decrypted content.
Idempotency/replay keys and watermarks are required; replay, expired requests, profile mismatch, and tombstone conflicts fail closed.
Local-arms/offline status stays honest and cannot imply local execution, bridge green, or model-processing proof.
Future server-originated handoff remains Architect-only; direct Builder/Oracle server targets are blocked.
Tier 3/Tier 4, customer-send, deploy, trade, financial, auth/secret, and local-execution requests remain blocked or review-required, never API-authorized.

Capability boundary prevents DB writes, storage, local execution, shell/PTY, Builder/Oracle direct server target, Tier 2+ action, customer-send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, model-processing proof, bridge-green claim, raw restore, and server resurrection of deleted state.

Real API server can become buildable later only after transport/TLS, auth middleware, replay store, rate limits, audit logging, schema migrations, encryption/KMS integration, deletion/export jobs, operator UI, monitoring/alerts, disaster recovery, profile isolation tests, and red-team leakage tests exist.
Acceptance gate IDs and blocker IDs are complete and unique.

Forbidden output substrings cover secrets, bearer tokens, cookies, key material, plaintext/ciphertext/decrypted content, raw private content, server/listener/network/write claims, execution overclaims, bridge/proof overclaims, direct Builder/Oracle target authorization, high-risk authorization overclaims, and live side-effect claims.
